headline: First-ever evidence-based baseline (Gen-2 forensic anchor #7 / Night 8): $230M combined DOJ civil + non-prosecution criminal settlement (Jan 2025) for 2014-2017 deceptive small-business credit card marketing — including dummy EINs, falsified income, unauthorized credit checks; plus active $17.5M antitrust settlement with claim deadline Apr 29, 2026; uniform 4×7+3.5×1=81.4 cluster invalidated for the seventh time
methodology_notes: F500 Gen-2 cluster 4×7+3.5×1=81.4 — seventh consecutive forensic anchor invalidating this cluster (TIAA -38.9, Masimo -33.0, BD -27.3, Danaher -26.7, General Mills -28.3, Eli Lilly -25.1, Cummins -28.3). AmEx delta of -25.9 is consistent with the Eli Lilly calibration (-25.1). INT 50.0 reflects a 3-year sustained deceptive marketing pattern (2014-2017) with criminal-side resolution (non-prosecution agreement + criminal fine + forfeiture of $138.4M) plus civil DOJ settlement of $108.7M. The criminal-side characterization (NPA rather than declination) is more severe than Eli Lilly's qui-tam petition because AmEx employees actually took action (dummy EINs '123456788', falsified income data, unauthorized credit checks); however INT held at 50.0 rather than lower because (a) Amex's 200-employee firing post-internal-investigation is documented self-remediation, and (b) the deceptive period ended ~2017, with subsequent governance reforms reported. ACC 43.8 reflects post-hoc accountability via fines plus self-disciplinary action but no admission of liability in the civil settlement. BND 50.0 is the secondary harm-distribution dimension: small-business customers received unauthorized credit checks (credit score harm), inaccurate tax advice (financial harm via Payroll/Premium Wire products), and were enrolled with dummy EINs into accounts they did not properly consent to. ACT/SYS 62.5 retained: AmEx's actual mission infrastructure — payments network, small-business cash flow products, travel-services support during disruptions — is genuinely positive. The active $17.5M antitrust settlement (anti-steering rules) is a secondary BND signal but is in a settlement posture without admission. Cluster-artifact invalidation now established through 7 consecutive anchors. Band change Exemplary → Functional.

evidence.claim: January 2025: American Express agreed to pay ~$230M to settle DOJ civil deceptive-marketing allegations and a parallel U.S. Attorney (E.D.N.Y.) wire-fraud investigation. Civil component: $108.7M with 4% annual interest. Criminal-side: non-prosecution agreement + $138.4M criminal fine and forfeiture.

Conduct 2014-2017: AmEx employees misrepresented card rewards/fees to small-business customers; conducted unauthorized credit checks; submitted falsified financial information including overstated business income; used dummy EINs (e.g., '123456788') to enroll customers without legally required identifiers (2015-1H 2016).

DOJ also alleged AmEx provided inaccurate tax advice via Payroll Rewards and Premium Wire products — marketed wiring fees as ordinary/necessary tax-deductible business expenses despite the fees not qualifying.

Active $17.5M anti-steering antitrust class settlement: claim deadline April 29, 2026; final approval hearing June 17, 2026. Allegations: AmEx prevented merchants from steering customers to lower-cost payment options, raising end-prices.
AmEx fired ~200 employees following internal investigation of the deceptive sales conduct — documented self-remediation that argues for INT 50.0 rather than lower.
Mission infrastructure: AmEx's payments network, small-business credit access (via legitimate channels), and travel-services restoration during disruptions support SYS 62.5 floor.

Published 81.4 is uniform 4×7+3.5×1 Gen-2 cluster profile shared with Cummins, Danaher, Eli Lilly, General Mills, U.S. Bancorp at F500 mid-ranks — invalidated as evidence-based by seven consecutive forensic anchors.

key_evidence: $230M combined DOJ settlement (Jan 2025) — $108.7M civil + $138.4M criminal NPA fine + forfeiture for 2014-2017 deceptive small-business credit card marketing
Conduct included dummy EINs ('123456788'), falsified income data, unauthorized credit checks, misrepresented rewards/fees, inaccurate tax advice on wire products
Active $17.5M antitrust class settlement (anti-steering) — claim deadline Apr 29, 2026 (in-window)
Genuine payments-network and small-business mission infrastructure supports SYS 62.5
Published 81.4 is uniform Gen-2 cluster invalidated by 7 prior forensic anchors
